title: Box office: 'Alien: Romulus' opens moderately with $41.5 million
text: 20th Century Studios released director Fede Álvarez's "Alien: Romulus" into 3,885 theaters, marking the first film in the long-running sci-fi horror franchise distributed by Disney. Starring Cailee Spaeny, the film earned a respectable 81% on Rotten Tomatoes and generated $6.5 million in Thursday previews. The movie made $18 million on Friday, including previews, which signaled a likely opening below $50 million. It ultimately earned $41.5 million for the weekend, becoming the franchise's second-best opening after 2012's "Prometheus" at $51 million. The film received a "B+" CinemaScore, surpassing the "B" ratings of prior installments "Prometheus" and "Alien: Covenant." Marvel's "Deadpool and Wolverine" dropped to second place with $29 million in its fourth weekend, down 46%, accumulating $545.8 million domestically and becoming the 17th highest-grossing film ever. "It Ends with Us" fell to third with $24 million, declining 52%, bringing its total to $97.8 million and approaching the $100 million milestone. "Twisters" landed fourth with $9.8 million, down 35%, reaching $238.4 million domestically and $333.4 million globally. A remastered 3D re-release of "Coraline" earned $8.4 million over the weekend, bringing its lifetime total to $86.6 million.
